canonical_uri.description:
Canonical URIs provide durable public identifiers for verifiable element types.
They are based on a namespace authority, project space, component group, and
element type.

compact_id.description:
Compact IDs provide human-readable and tool-friendly identifiers. Authority
aliases must be resolved through the namespace authority registry.

cross_artifact_consistency.description:
Each AR-owned identifier should have exactly one authoritative definition.
Prose documents, schemas, examples, exports, and implementations may cite
identifiers, but those references must not create competing definitions.

hosting.description:
Hosting metadata may identify GitHub, GitLab, institutional repositories,
private registries, or other locations. Hosting changes must not change
canonical identity.

persistent_id.description:
Persistent IDs provide optional non-HTTP identifiers for archival,
proprietary, internal, or non-web contexts.

versioned_reference.description:
Versioned references identify a specific released version of an element type,
element package, schema, or compatible artifact.
